title: AEPDecisionLedgerEntry
description: Normalized governance record for every AEP evolution attempt. Provides durable evolutionary memory to prevent cyclic optimization and enable deterministic ledger lookup before re-attempting interventions.

entry_id.description: Content-addressed identifier derived from target_subsystem, observed_failure, proposed_intervention, verdict, and simulation metrics. Two proposals with identical inputs produce the same ID, making duplicates detectable and historical lookup deterministic.

timestamp_utc.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the entry was recorded.

target_subsystem.description: EvolvableComponent ladder position targeted by this proposal.

observed_failure.description: Description of the failure that motivated this proposal.

justification_level.description: Intervention ladder classification derived from the target component. L1_CONFIG = smallest change (RETRIEVAL, MEMORY_POLICY); L4_CORE_MODEL = largest change (BASE_MODEL).

verdict.description: Governance outcome: PROMOTED = improved and deployed; REJECTED = governance declined (no simulation needed or no improvement observed); BLOCKED = simulation ran and failed safety/performance gate.

simulation_metrics.description: Present when simulation ran (PROMOTED and BLOCKED verdicts). Absent for REJECTED verdicts where the ladder check fired before simulation.

baseline_error_rate.description: Error rate of the unmodified system on the replay window. Lower is better.

simulated_error_rate.description: Error rate of the proposed change on the same replay window. Lower is better.

simulation_verified.description: True when the proposal passed through AEP simulation before governance evaluation.

tampered_deploy_attempt_detected.description: True when an out-of-band deploy attempt was detected during this governance cycle.

execution_hash.description: SHA-256 of the simulation execution context (component, change, ledgerRef, error rates, label, outcome). Enables replay verification.

causal_signature.description: Optional causal context enabling clustering of related failures. The same intervention may appear under different surface symptoms (e.g. slow retrieval and tool timeout both caused by index degradation). A causal signature allows deduplication and cross-failure analysis.

failure_class.description: Classification of the originating failure (e.g. RETRIEVAL_PRECISION_DEGRADATION).

affected_metric.description: The measured metric that was degraded (e.g. execution_latency).

origin_event_hash.description: Hash of the originating RIG ledger event.

context_metadata.description: Free-form key-value pairs describing the evaluation environment at the time this entry was recorded. Used by AEP-MEM-002 to compute a context delta when a previously vetoed intervention is re-proposed. Absence of this field is treated as identical context — an entry without context_metadata cannot have its veto evaporated.

resolution_context.description: Present when this entry overrules a prior veto via AEP-MEM-002 contradiction resolution. Creates a first-class relationship between the new decision and the historical entry it supersedes. The historical entry is not modified — both entries coexist as an immutable causal chain.

resolution_type.description: Classification of the contradiction resolution strategy. SIMULATOR_INVALIDATION: old verdict was an artifact of a flawed or outdated simulator. CONTEXTUAL_BIFURCATION: intervention targets a different domain than the historical failure. BOUNDARY_SHIFT: an environmental dependency or acceptance threshold has moved.

overruled_entry_id.description: entry_id of the historical BLOCKED or REJECTED entry whose veto was evaporated by context change.

context_delta.description: Changed context fields that justified evaporating the historical veto. Each key maps to an old/new pair.

old.description: Context field value at the time of the historical (vetoed) entry.

new.description: Context field value at the time of this (overriding) entry.

evidence_required.description: When true, the system requires both a changed context delta AND new simulation evidence to promote an overriding entry. Prevents context change alone from being sufficient justification.
